close_reason: Fixed: documented unrestricted-key-only limitation in module docs
description: The null HashcheckTicket (hierarchy=NULL, empty digest) is only valid for unrestricted signing keys. If the persistent handle points to a restricted signing key, the TPM returns TPM_RC_TICKET, which map_tpm_error classifies as a permanent Backend error. The error message is a raw TSS2 error string with no hint that the root cause is a restricted-key/unrestricted-key mismatch. Since the crate cannot change TPM behavior, the fix is documentation: add a note to the sign() doc-comment and the module-level docs that the key handle must be an unrestricted signing key (TPMA_OBJECT.restricted = 0). Alternatively, read_public() before signing and check the restricted attribute, returning a clear error.
title: sign() fails opaquely on restricted keys
